lesson: Icy product with high beater amps but refrigeration perfectly in spec means the scraper blades are worn down and ice is dragging the beater. The cheap blade swap is the fix everyone overlooks while quoting a new compressor.
symptoms: Product coming out icy and soft.

tests.error-log: No active errors. One historical high-torque warning, three days old.
tests.temp-probe: Barrel temp in spec. Hopper temp in spec.
tests.inspect-beater: Scraper blades are worn down to nubs. Ice is building up on the barrel wall.

symptoms: Serve texture gritty with ice flecks.
Machine louder than usual under load.
Manager says it's been creeping up for weeks.

tests.error-log: Intermittent BEATER TORQUE HIGH warnings, more frequent every week for a month.
tests.inspect-beater: Blade edges chamfered round — a rind of hard ice on the barrel wall they're no longer shaving.

symptoms: Overrun collapsed — product coming out dense and wet.
Beater audibly labouring during every draw.

tests.error-log: No active errors. Motor current trend has drifted upward for six weeks straight.
tests.inspect-beater: Scraper blades worn to nubs. Ice build-up on the barrel wall dragging the beater.

flavour: The $15 fix everyone overlooks while quoting a new compressor.
authenticityNote: Worn scraper blades stop shaving the barrel wall, so ice accumulates and drags the beater — icy product plus high motor amps with refrigeration perfectly healthy. A genuinely classic cheap fix.
